tax: Cataract Spume is placed at species rank beneath Spume (Fluvial order, Water Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on an aerated white-water body, a battering-and-drowning churn, and collapse to ordinary flow when dispersed. It is separated from its sibling the Sump Surger by its aerated white churn at falls versus the Surger's dark still surge from depth; the two share the genus body-plan but never the still-water surge. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: aerated, fast-moving water held coherent at a cascade.
ap: Cataract Spume stands 2–4 m churning mass at a fall. Its silhouette is a roaring vortex of foam clinging to a cascade. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: a churning white foam-body brighter than the surrounding flow; a roar pitched differently from the natural fall, rising as it rouses; and driven spray thrown against the current rather than with it. It moves with anchored to its fall, able to surge a short way up or down the cascade but not leave running water, and shows none; pale with foam. Even after dispersal it can be identified from an unusually scoured plunge-pool and spray-wetted stone above the natural splash-line, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Cataract Spume persists in cataracts, cascades, and falls of the running-water passages, where aerated moving water sustains it. It does not breed; it forms where water-pressure animates the aerated churn at a cascade, and recurs whenever the fall keeps running and the churn re-coheres after dispersal. It sustains its mass on kinetic energy of falling water, drawing in the cascade's own flow, drawn into its churn. Range is bounded by the length of its cascade: outside that band the body collapses to ordinary flow away from the fall. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a fall whose foam churns against the current and roars off-pitch, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Cataract Spume is roused by anything entering its plunge-pool or crossing near its fall and otherwise lies it cannot truly rest while the fall runs; it churns on. It claims a single cascade or fall and answers intrusion with dragging prey into the churn to batter and drown. Toward grey-folk it is hazard to anyone fording near a fall; survey crews report that Stillwardens rope crossings well below a roaring fall, never at the plunge-pool. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Cataract Spume: a body of aerated, fast-moving water held coherent at a cascade; immune to cutting and bludgeoning, which pass through foam; and a defining weakness — freezing and diversion of its fall, which starve it. Its senses run through the medium itself: motion and pressure through the churn. It tolerates darkness, cold short of freezing, and depth but fails at freezing and the diversion or drying of its fall. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the foam-body and fall-anchored churn, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Cataract Spume are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it drags prey into a battering, drowning churn; it throws driven spray to blind and unbalance; it scours and undercuts footing at its plunge-pool; and it surges a short way up or down its cascade to reach prey. Each is a direct expression of the kinetic churn of aerated falling water, so it cannot, for instance, leave running water, produce heat, or hold form when frozen. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Cataract Spume matters because it makes fords near falls lethal, and crews now rope crossings well below any roaring plunge-pool. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a drowning-and-battering hazard logged with falls and cascades, and its presence reshapes routes through the cascade fords, now roped low and never crossed at the plunge-pool. A fall whose foam roars off-pitch and throws spray upstream marks a roused Spume.
g: Cataract Spume (Unda spumans) is a white-water elemental that churns at waterfalls and cascades, drowning and battering in aerated foam. Documented from the cataracts, cascades, and falls of the running-water passages, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a roaring body of aerated white water and driven spray — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. Where the Surger is dark, still, and rising, the Spume is white, falling, and battering, born of moving water. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a sudden clearing of the foam and a return to ordinary cascade flow and no carcass.
